title: Hardcoded, low-entropy JWT signing secret committed in source enables token forgery and full privilege escalation

cat: Hardcoded credential / API token in source control
title: Live Git Personal Access Token hardcoded in committed config template (and live git password recoverable from Dockerfile history)

cat: Private key / TLS keystore in source control
title: TLS keystore (server.jks) and DER private key committed to source control with plaintext keystore password; .gitignore inert
Hardcoded ShareFile production credentials and OAuth client secret committed in source
CORS allows any origin reflected with credentials (allowCredentials=true + allowedOriginPattern '*')
Outbound TLS certificate AND hostname validation fully disabled in Git, License, and container git clients (trust-all)
OS command injection via git CLI: repo name and release tags interpolated into a shell command stream
SOQL injection in Salesforce contact lookup (ticket email concatenated into query)
No object-level / tenant authorization on resource-by-id endpoints (IDOR), and the @PreAuthorize target (#targert) is misspelled so object checks are dead code
Service-identity audiences grant fixed high privileges purely from a client-supplied JWT audience claim; refresh endpoint re-issues tokens from an unverified expired token
Full response bodies logged at INFO for every consumer API endpoint — reset tokens, JWTs & PII leak to log files
Pipeline packages git-tracked secrets (config dir) into the deploy artifact uploaded to S3
Overly broad wildcard IAM policies (admin actions + iam:PassRole *) in CloudFormation pipeline IaC
Edge reverse proxy serves only plaintext HTTP (no TLS, no HTTP->HTTPS redirect) and proxies upstreams over http://
PostgreSQL connection uses NonValidatingFactory — TLS without certificate validation (DB MITM)
Jasypt configured with weak PBEWithMD5AndDES algorithm and low iteration count for secrets at rest
Security tokens and package passwords generated with non-cryptographic PRNG (RandomStringUtils / java.util.Random)
Actuator endpoints permitAll (no auth); dead management.security.enabled=false and static admin/secret credentials committed across all environments
No account lockout / brute-force throttling or rate limiting on login, password-reset, or any endpoint
JWT access token, 30-day refresh token, and user PII persisted to browser localStorage (XSS-exfiltratable)
nb-markdown renders user-authored markdown to innerHTML WITHOUT sanitization on the readonly path (stored DOM XSS)

cat: Data Confidentiality / Insecure Direct Object Access
title: S3 pre-signed download URLs hardcoded to 6-hour validity, ignoring configured link-expiry policy
Hardcoded GitHub webhook HMAC secret 'netbrain', non-constant-time comparison, and signature logging
fastjson 1.2.83 (EOL) and EOL/vulnerable frontend production dependencies shipped to production
No SAST quality gate, SCA, or secret scanning in either CI pipeline
Deployment compose pulls application images from personal Docker Hub namespaces; EOL/unpinned base images; containers run as root
Customer support-ticket PII has no enforced deletion/retention despite a 'delete when resolved' flag; expired tokens never purged
Security events not audited: failed logins, authorization denials, logout, and all administrative IAM changes are unrecorded
No centralized/tamper-resistant log aggregation; audit log is a mutable JPA entity
Live Salesforce OAuth access token and customer license keys logged in clear text (production paths)
No application-level rate limiting / login throttling enables DoS and brute-force (availability)
Plaintext DB passwords ('Netbrain!', postgres) hardcoded in docker-compose files and packaged application.properties

cat: Hardcoded encryption key in source control
title: Jasypt master passwords committed next to their ciphertext in test resources/code
Password complexity enforced only client-side; backend accepts any password on create/update/reset
Password-reset flow enables user/email enumeration and tokens are not single-use on verify (PII disclosed on public verify)
Write/sync operations lack method-level authorization; permission check uses substring match instead of equality
Systemic missing request-body validation: @RequestBody without @Valid and DTO constraints commented out
Path traversal sinks: Zip Slip in CommonUtils.unzip and uploaded-filename path in framework-release upload
Custom SMTP send path enables TLS without enforcing certificate validation or requiring STARTTLS
Package signing only covers the first N bytes of the upgrade package (truncated integrity)
Unauthenticated actuator, API-docs/swagger, and webhook endpoints removed from the security filter chain (web.ignoring + permitAll)
Server-side root-cause exception messages returned to clients; verbose stack traces and printStackTrace() in production
Audited data-export/download actions attributed to licenseId/robot instead of the authenticated user; UI reads not audited
Unbounded 4GB multipart upload limit against a 25-connection pool enables resource-exhaustion DoS
S3 object uploads do not specify server-side encryption
Frontend nginx serves no Content-Security-Policy or security response headers (and app filter omits Permissions-Policy/frame-ancestors and skips the management port)

cat: PII in Non-Prod Fixtures / Data Minimization
title: GitHub webhook DTO/data fixtures and seed data embed PII-shaped values, reused weak hashes, and a committed Jasypt master password
Outdated backend libraries shipped to production (gson 2.8.5, httpcore/httpclient 4.x, reactor-core pin, saaj/jaxb) plus unpinned RELEASE metaversion
CI build/change-management gaps: tests skipped, unsigned artifacts, npm ci --force, OIDC token in world-readable /tmp, reused credential, empty prod approver
ShareFile getItemByPath builds URL with unencoded user path (URL/parameter injection)
Templated support email injects unescaped user-controlled values (HTML/email content injection)
Weak/legacy cryptography: MD5 file hashing and hand-rolled RSA license signature verification

cat: Embedded key material in source control
title: RSA license-verification public key embedded in committed config template alongside live secrets
Frontend defense-in-depth gaps: bearer token attached to any host, open-redirect returnUrl, untrusted content via [innerHTML], guid() uses Math.random()
Frontend authorization (route guard + nb-permissions + tenant interceptor) is UI-only and client-trusted
PII (email/displayName) and customer license data written to local application logs
Vulnerable/EOL frontend test & build tooling and transitive dev dependencies
Very old test-scope libraries pinned across modules (Mockito 2.23.4; nb-kc-main JUnit 4.12 = CVE-2020-15250)
Inconsistent/undocumented mTLS boundary configuration across profiles

cat: Embedded credential in test data / hygiene
title: Trivial credential-like literals and onboarding screenshots committed (informational; no live secrets)
